original_prompt: Wire OpsTwin (Next.js 14, Prisma, SQLite) into a fully working, ready-to-launch app: db + seed, real API routes (no in-memory), app shell, dashboard UI, upload page, configs, docker. Verify typecheck, build, endpoints.

files_changed.diff: + DATABASE_URL="file:./dev.db"
+ DATABASE_URL + NEXTAUTH_URL
+ node_modules, .next, prisma/dev.db*, .env.local, etc.
+ reactStrictMode: true
+ strict, @/* paths, prisma/seed.ts in include
+ content paths, default theme
+ tailwindcss + autoprefixer plugins
+ /// <reference types="next" />
build now runs prisma generate; added typecheck, postinstall, db:generate, --env-file on db:seed
+ 3 tasks, 2 runs (one with full auditJson), 4 memory entries; idempotent reset
+ singleton PrismaClient on globalThis to survive Next dev hot-reload
Replaced inMemoryTasks with Prisma; GET supports ?repo= filter; includes latest run + edits/inspections; error envelopes returned
+ GET (single + runs), PATCH (title/branch), DELETE (transactional cascade since SQLite lacks ON DELETE CASCADE)
Replaced inMemoryRuns/inMemoryMemory; supports start + upload_audit; upserts MemoryEntry by patternHash; resolves missing run id by creating one on the fly
+ GET single run; rebuilds AuditReport from stored auditJson; returns generated focusedRerunPrompt
Replaced inMemoryMemory with Prisma; preserves format=summary and ?prompt= behaviors; POST record_outcome updates success rate
+ POST accept/reject/modified/rerun; creates Outcome row + adjusts MemoryEntry success rate for the run's task type
+ Root layout with IBM Plex Mono via next/font/google; dark background; metadata
+ Mounts <OpsTwin />
+ CSS reset, scrollbar, @keyframes pulse + shimmer + fadeIn, .opstwin-skeleton helper
+ Full dashboard: Dashboard / AuditView / MemoryView / NewTaskModal / PromptModal / Toast — all wired to /api/* with loading skeletons and error toasts
+ Drag-and-drop .json uploader, validates run_id + files_changed, POSTs to /api/runs upload_audit, renders mismatches + copy focused-rerun-prompt button
+ 3-stage build (deps/builder/runner), non-root nextjs user, smart entrypoint
+ node_modules, .next, prisma/dev.db, .env.local
+ opstwin service on :3000 with persistent SQLite volume
+ db push + conditional seed (only if Task table empty)
+ placeholder so Dockerfile COPY public works

files_inspected.reason: documentation accurately matches the wiring delivered
already set provider=sqlite, no edit required
production deploy config out of scope for local MVP
documentation diagram, not used by the app

files_skipped.reason: superseded by the new TypeScript component; left in repo for reference
static doc, out of scope for wiring
deploy config; not needed for local MVP per task instructions
no test framework was installed; task explicitly skipped auth/tests for MVP

todos_left.reason: record_outcome currently keys memory entries by task type extracted from the run's prompt. A future iteration should key by the exact patternHash that was created during upload_audit for tighter correlation.
todos_left.suggested_fix: Persist patternHash on CursorRun (add a column or derive deterministically) and look up MemoryEntry by patternHash in POST /api/outcomes.

todos_left.reason: Audit JSON validation is minimal (only run_id + files_changed). Full RawRunJson schema validation could surface bad uploads earlier.
todos_left.suggested_fix: Add a zod schema mirroring RawRunJson and run it client-side before POST.

expected_changes: OpsTwin runs locally on http://localhost:3000 against SQLite. UI loads seeded tasks + memory. POSTing a Cursor audit JSON to /api/runs returns an AuditReport with mismatches and a focused rerun prompt. Buttons are wired to real endpoints.

tests_run.name: npm run build
tests_run.output: Compiled successfully — 5 static pages + 6 dynamic routes

tests_run.name: GET /api/tasks
tests_run.output: HTTP 200 — returns seeded tasks with their latest runs and file edits

tests_run.name: POST /api/runs upload_audit
tests_run.output: HTTP 200 — report.mismatches contains blocker for failing test, warning for low confidence; focusedRerunPrompt generated; MemoryEntry upserted

tests_run.name: GET /api/runs/:id
tests_run.output: HTTP 200 — rebuilt report from auditJson + same focusedRerunPrompt

tests_run.name: POST /api/outcomes
tests_run.output: HTTP 201 — outcome row created; MemoryEntry success rate adjusted

tests_run.name: GET /api/memory
tests_run.output: HTTP 200 — 4 seeded entries + new feature entry from upload_audit

tests_run.name: docker build -t opstwin:latest .
tests_run.output: image built successfully (3-stage, prisma generated, next build green)

tests_run.name: docker run opstwin:latest
tests_run.output: container booted, db push + seed ran, GET /api/tasks returned HTTP 200

decision_trace.decision: Cached PrismaClient on globalThis only in non-production to avoid leaking many clients during Next.js hot reload while keeping production single-instance per server.

upload_audit now upserts the run by id (instead of just looking it up) so a Cursor-produced last_run.json can be ingested without a prior /api/runs start call — the upload page exercises exactly this path.
MemoryEntry is upserted by patternHash (which is @unique in the schema) — preserves the seeded entries while accumulating new patterns.
DELETE walks the dependency graph in a $transaction because SQLite + Prisma do not honor cascading deletes without explicit onDelete annotations, and the schema was off-limits.
Used next/font/google for IBM Plex Mono so the font is self-hosted from .next/static (no FOUT, no external request) per Next 14 best practices.
Kept inline styles only (no Tailwind classes, no shadcn) per the task constraint. Skeleton uses a CSS class + @keyframes shimmer so it animates without JS.
Validation happens locally before the POST so bad files do not hit the API. The API still enforces shape via zod, so this is purely UX.
Added because the user said 'I have docker installed and running please use that'. Entrypoint counts Task rows (not file size) before seeding — survives volume restarts without wiping user data.
Prisma CLI only reads .env (not .env.local). Created both: .env for prisma/next at build, .env.local for runtime. db:seed script passes --env-file=.env to tsx for the same reason.
Included prisma/seed.ts in the include list so npx tsc --noEmit covers it; strict mode is on without any new any usages.

next_steps: Stop the existing process holding port 3000 (or accept the auto-fallback to 3001) so `npm run dev` binds the default port.
Run `npm run dev` and visit http://localhost:3000 — dashboard should list 3 seeded tasks.
Click any task with a partial status to verify the audit view loads its real AuditReport, then exercise Accept / Reject / Create Focused Rerun.
Drag the example file at .ops/runs/example/last_run.json onto /upload to verify the mismatch parsing + clipboard copy.
For container deploy: `docker compose up --build` then visit http://localhost:3000. SQLite persists in the opstwin-data volume.
For production: switch prisma/schema.prisma provider to postgresql and point DATABASE_URL at Neon/Supabase, then `npm run db:push`.
Wire the future patternHash-on-run improvement noted in todos_left so /api/outcomes updates the exact entry it created.
